metadata.audit_tier: Tier 1 static HTML/JSX analysis. Tier 2 live browser + NVDA walkthrough required to verify announcement timing and focus order.
metadata.version_delta: v0.1.0 -> v0.2.0 adds: tour, spotlight, F10/Menu, demo kata, gamepad, F2 rename

summary.score_revision_note: Original 68 assumed Space block was a WCAG violation. Revised to 72 after confirming Space block is intentional design backed by NVDA browse-mode research (journal 2026-03-24). After Tier A + B fixes (17 items), final score 80.

categories.name: Keyboard Navigation
categories.note: C3 focus trap added + Space block reclassified

categories.name: Cognitive
categories.note: W9 SR announcement on Escape-to-back-button added

categories.name: Agent Operability
categories.note: TD1 dead code removed, TD2a duplicate contentinfo fixed

findings.title: Space key intentionally blocked — documentation-only improvement
findings.compliance_status: intentional design trade-off, WCAG compliant via Enter
findings.affected_users: Keyboard users expecting Space activation (minor UX friction)
findings.description: INITIAL AUDIT MISCLASSIFIED THIS AS CRITICAL. Verified via git blame + vault journal 2026-03-24 addendum [01:30] that Space block is an intentional defense against NVDA browse-mode synthetic clicks. NVDA in browse mode translates Space into a synthetic click event (detail=1, indistinguishable from a mouse click); with default focus on TRY, this would bypass the 3-second long-press friction and launch a game unintentionally. Defense-in-depth: default focus was also moved from TRY to NOT NOW so that if anything slips through, the worst case is 'skip' not 'launch'. WCAG 2.1.1 Level A is satisfied because Enter provides full keyboard access (short press = visit, hold = anchor). The code comment was misleading ('avoid NVDA+Space conflict' suggests a keybinding collision, but the real reason is synthetic click defense) — now corrected in code with reference to this audit + journal.
findings.fix: Code comment now documents the real reasoning. Add a 'Known limitations' bullet to AccessibilityPage explaining Space behavior so users know to use Enter. No code behavior change required.
findings.legal_exposure: Low. WCAG 2.1.1 Level A is satisfied via Enter. Documented limitation is defensible.
revision_history: 2026-04-12 initial audit: flagged as critical based on assumption that NVDA uses NVDA+Space only
2026-04-12 revised: after reading git blame + journal, reclassified as intentional design with documentation gap

findings.title: role="alert" fires on every re-render, spamming NVDA
findings.affected_users: All screen reader users (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver)
findings.description:
{t('ui.rin.sr_guide')}

is rendered inside main. role="alert" is equivalent to aria-live="assertive" with role="alertdialog" semantics — it interrupts whatever NVDA is reading. Because this element is rendered on every mount/remount (and RinView re-mounts on game change, locale change, etc), the SR guide is interruptively announced repeatedly. Intended as a one-time onboarding hint, it becomes a nuisance.
findings.fix: Either (a) render the hint only once via useRef gate, or (b) replace role="alert" with role="status" aria-live="polite" so it announces politely on first mount only. Prefer (a) with localStorage persistence so returning users never hear it.
findings.legal_exposure: WCAG 4.1.3 AA. Not typically litigated but degrades assistive tech UX severely.

findings.title: GuidedTour has role="dialog" but no focus trap
findings.wcag: ARIA Authoring Practices dialog pattern; WCAG 2.4.3
findings.affected_users: Screen reader + keyboard users
findings.description: The tour is declared role="dialog" with aria-label, but Tab can escape to the UI elements underneath the spotlight overlay (clip-path creates a visual hole but DOM remains tab-reachable). Per ARIA Authoring Practices dialog pattern, focus must be trapped inside the dialog. Additionally, aria-live="polite" on the dialog is an unusual combination: dialog itself is not a live region. When the step text changes, this causes NVDA to re-announce the entire dialog content on every step, while focus also moves to the Next button — resulting in double announcement (live region update + focus change).
findings.fix: (1) Add Tab trap: on keydown Tab inside dialog, cycle focus among Skip/Prev/Next (or interactive target). Handle Shift+Tab reverse. (2) Remove aria-live="polite" from dialog. Instead, on step change, move focus to the new Next button (already done) — SR will announce via focus change, not via live region.
findings.legal_exposure: WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order A. Custom dialog without trap is a recurring ADA complaint.
findings.code_after:
...

{formatHotkeys(step?.text)}

findings.title: Spotlight hint text is unreachable by screen readers
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users
findings.description: The help-spotlight-overlay has aria-hidden="true" on the outer div, which correctly hides the decorative clip-path effect — but it also hides the .help-spotlight-hint paragraph that contains the onboarding message ('press the ? button for help'). The spotlight is intended as a visual affordance for first-time users. Sighted users see the pulse + arrow + text; SR users get nothing. Although focus is moved to the help button (SR announces its aria-label), the contextual 'this is a help spotlight' meaning is lost.
findings.fix: Render the hint text outside the aria-hidden overlay, or use a separate sr-only region with role="status" that announces the spotlight's purpose when it appears. Alternatively, attach aria-describedby from the help button to the hint element (the hint must not be aria-hidden for this to work).
findings.legal_exposure: WCAG 1.3.1 A (information not conveyed by non-text alone).
findings.code_after:
{t('ui.tour.spotlight_hint')}

↑ {t('ui.tour.spotlight_hint')}

findings.title: TRY button long-press has no live progress announcement
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users, cognitive disabilities
findings.description: The TRY button has a progress bar inside it (role="progressbar" aria-valuenow updating), but NVDA typically only reads progressbar values on explicit focus — and the progressbar is nested inside the button which already has focus. So as the user holds Enter for 3 seconds, they get no feedback that progress is happening. Sighted users see the progress fill; haptic users feel vibration; SR users get silence. The button's aria-label says 'hold 3 seconds' but never confirms when the anchor completes. On long-press completion, the game anchors — but the state transition is only announced if the game title changes (it does: prepends ⚓).
findings.fix: Add a separate sr-only aria-live="polite" region that announces progress milestones (e.g. at 50% and 100%): 'anchoring in 2 seconds', 'anchoring in 1 second', 'anchored'. Keep the visual progressbar for sighted users.
findings.legal_exposure: WCAG 4.1.3 AA (status messages must be programmatically determinable).

findings.title: Window-focus event forcibly steals focus back to primary button
findings.affected_users: Keyboard + screen reader users who Alt-Tab between apps
findings.description: Multiple views register a window 'focus' event listener that forces focus back to a primary button whenever the window regains focus. For a user who Tab-navigated to the Footer legal links or a Kata row, then Alt-Tabbed to check an email and came back, their focus position is silently reset. The only guard is 'if document.activeElement is body or null' in RinView — but Onboarding and FrozenScreen steal unconditionally (with only mount-time guard).
findings.fix: Only force-focus when document.activeElement is null or document.body — i.e., when focus is genuinely lost. Never override a valid active element. RinView already does this; apply the same guard to OnboardingView and FrozenScreen.
findings.legal_exposure: WCAG 3.2.1 On Focus A (unexpected focus change).

findings.title: F10 hijacks the OS-standard menu activation key
findings.affected_users: Keyboard power users, NVDA users familiar with Windows conventions
findings.description: Windows convention: F10 activates the application menu bar (File menu, etc.). Tauri apps can choose to expose a native menu, but Maida has no native menu — so F10 having a custom binding is defensible. However, users with muscle memory press F10 expecting 'menu', and the action here does open Settings (consistent with the concept of 'menu'). The risk is minimal but discoverability is low. This is a design trade-off, not a bug, but should be documented in AccessibilityPage.
findings.fix: Accept as-is but document in the AccessibilityPage under 'Limitations' or 'Custom shortcuts'. Consider also accepting the Windows Menu key (AppsKey / ContextMenu key) as an alternative that does not collide with F10.
findings.legal_exposure: Low. Not a clear violation; platform convention is soft.
findings.code_after:
if (e.key === 'F10' || e.key === 'ContextMenu') { e.preventDefault(); openSettings(); }
// And: document in AccessibilityPage

findings.title: Onboarding intro text may be skipped on landing
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users on first launch
findings.description: On mount, focus moves to the h1 (tabIndex=-1). NVDA announces 'Maida, heading level 1'. Then the user Tab-navigates to the sync button. But the intro paragraph (permission_intro + permission_detail) lives between h1 and button in DOM order — it is skipped unless the user explicitly arrow-navigates. The first-time onboarding message is critical and should not require explicit exploration to discover.
findings.fix: Add aria-describedby to the main element pointing to the intro paragraph(s). Alternatively, use a role="status" aria-live="polite" wrapper on the paragraph so it auto-announces after h1. The simplest: wrap the intro paragraphs in a region labeled as description of the main.
findings.code_after:
Maida

{intro}

{detail}

findings.title: Kata delete 2-click confirmation has no SR feedback on first click
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users creating katas
findings.description: The delete button changes aria-label on second state (confirmDeleteId). But on first click, the button does NOT move focus or announce anything — the user only sees a visual state change (× becomes ?). NVDA would not announce the state change because aria-label updates while focused don't automatically re-announce (NVDA only re-reads on focus-in). Users unaware of the confirm pattern may repeatedly click the button expecting deletion, or get confused.
findings.fix: On first click, announce via aria-live: 'press again to confirm delete'. Alternatively, move focus away and back to force re-read.

findings.title: Scanning state in Onboarding is not announced
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users during Steam scan
findings.description: When state transitions from 'idle' to 'scanning', the visible indicator appears but has no aria-live. The .onboarding-voice paragraph retains its previous text (intro) because the ternary only switches between error and intro — scanning is unhandled. If scanning takes more than a second, SR users are left with no audible confirmation that anything happened.
findings.fix: Add a dedicated aria-live region for the scanning state, or extend the voice ternary to include a scanning message.

findings.title: Custom focus tracking via focusin/focusout instead of :focus-visible
findings.affected_users: All keyboard users
findings.description: Both views implement document-level focusin/focusout listeners that set state.isFocused, then apply a CSS class .is-focused. This duplicates what :focus-visible does natively, and introduces state-sync bugs (setTimeout(0) race conditions visible in handleFocusOut). The comments don't explain why :focus-visible was rejected — likely to keep the focused state persistent even after clicking elsewhere. If that is the requirement, document it. Otherwise the existing 24 :focus-visible usages elsewhere are the right pattern.
findings.fix: Either migrate to :focus-visible (simplest) or add a comment explaining why the custom tracker is needed (e.g. 'keep focus ring visible after mouse-click so gamepad users see the landing spot').
findings.legal_exposure: Low — focus indicator is present, just mechanism is non-standard.

findings.title: Pending language selection has no SR hint about 2-click confirmation
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users changing language
findings.description: Clicking a language button sets pending state; clicking again commits. Visual hint 'Click again to confirm' is rendered but without aria-live — SR users only hear the button label, not the pending instruction. The button's aria-pressed only reflects current (committed) language, not pending.
findings.fix: Add role="status" aria-live="polite" region near the language list announcing pending state. Or append the pending hint to the button's accessible name when pending.

findings.title: Two-step Back (B/Escape) in Settings is undiscoverable
findings.affected_users: Gamepad + keyboard users
findings.description: Escape in Settings first focuses the back button, then closes on second press. There is no visual or SR indication of this two-step pattern. Users expect Escape to close; they get no feedback that focus moved.
findings.fix: Announce the focus move to back button via sr-only aria-live, OR simplify to single-Escape-closes. Two-step is protective against accidental dismiss but should be taught on first encounter.

findings.title: Fixed 100vh body with overflow: hidden may break reflow at 320px
findings.affected_users: Users zooming to 200%, low-resolution Tauri windows
findings.description: body has overflow: hidden, width: 100vw, height: 100vh. This pattern is common in Tauri but it prevents content from reflowing to a single column at 320px — content gets cut off rather than scrolled. Tauri minimum window size should be checked in tauri.conf.json to ensure content never renders below 320px effective width.
findings.fix: Either (a) set tauri.conf.json minWidth to a value that guarantees content fits, or (b) allow body overflow-y: auto as fallback when content exceeds viewport height. Also unverifiable because this cannot be tested without live browser — flag for Tier 2 audit.

findings.title: Controls tables have no <caption>
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users navigating settings
findings.description: The three controls tables (Rin/Kamae/General) have <thead> and <th> but no <caption>. Screen readers announce tables better with captions. Currently the preceding h3 provides context (via aria-describedby pattern), but caption is the semantic default.
findings.fix:
...
{t('ui.settings.controls_rin')}
— and remove the standalone h3 to avoid duplication, or keep h3 for visual and add sr-only caption.

findings.title: Font sizes in px violate project's own rem convention
findings.affected_users: Users with custom browser font sizes
findings.description: Tunable font vars are in px (--prescription-size: 22px, etc). User's own font-size-consolidation rule specifies all px should be rem. This prevents OS/browser-level text scaling from applying.
findings.fix: Convert to rem: --prescription-size: 1.375rem, etc. Side benefit: if body font-size is adjustable (currently inherits browser default 16px), all scales uniformly.

findings.title: Footer links are the only path to AccessibilityPage
findings.affected_users: Anyone looking for the accessibility statement
findings.description: AccessibilityPage exists (good!) but the only entry point is Footer links. Convention for accessibility statements is to place them in the Settings/About area as well. Adding a Settings shortcut would improve discoverability.
findings.fix: Add 'Accessibility' row to SettingsPanel that opens AccessibilityPage via setLegalPage('accessibility'). This works well given F10 opens Settings — one keystroke to reach the accessibility statement.

findings.title: console.log calls remain in production input hook
findings.affected_users: None directly, but indicates dev-debug leakage
findings.description: Many console.log statements in the input hook fire on every press/release. Not an a11y issue but floods the devtools console. If users report SR bugs and copy devtools output, the logs obscure signal.
findings.fix: Wrap in import.meta.env.DEV guard or remove entirely before v0.3.0.

findings.title: formatHotkeys regex only matches 「」 quoted names
findings.affected_users: English + Japanese locale users (zh-TW works)
findings.description: formatHotkeys splits on 「[^」]+」 (Chinese corner brackets) — so English button names in quotes are not wrapped in <kbd>. Minor semantic loss. SR users still hear the text, just without the kbd semantics.
findings.fix: Extend regex: /(Ctrl\+Tab|LB|RB|F1|F2|「[^」]+」|"[^"]+"|'[^']+')/g

findings.title: Dead code: KernelView.jsx + InterfaceView.jsx (298 lines, pre-v0.1.0)
findings.affected_users: Maintainers; indirectly users if re-imported
findings.description: Both files predate v0.1.0 (last touched 2026-03-28) and have zero imports. They contain hardcoded English strings with no i18n, bare <button> without aria-label, and outdated design language ('kernel'/'interface' were abandoned in favor of 'rin'/'kamae'). Dead code is tech debt: increases review burden, risks accidental re-import, bloats bundle (if tree-shaking misses).
findings.fix: rm src/views/KernelView.jsx src/views/KernelView.css src/views/InterfaceView.jsx src/views/InterfaceView.css

findings.title: Duplicate role="contentinfo" landmarks, one nested inside <main>
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users navigating by landmark
findings.description: Two elements carry role='contentinfo': the global-version-tag div (App.jsx) and the Footer element. Both also render inside <main> (Footer is nested via RinView/KamaeView). ARIA landmark pattern expects one contentinfo per page, sibling to main. NVDA's D-key landmark navigation enumerates both, confusing the landmark structure. Separately, global-version-tag is not semantically contentinfo (it holds app version + update alert).
findings.fix: (1) Remove role='contentinfo' from global-version-tag. (2) Move <Footer /> out of each view's <main> and render at app-root level in App.jsx.

findings.title: ErrorBoundary is English-only, no role=alert, hardcoded colors, destructive button without confirm
findings.affected_users: All users when app crashes (JP/zh users get English; SR users get no announcement; theme users get hardcoded dark)
findings.description: Four interconnected issues: (1) hardcoded 'Maida encountered an error.' with no i18n. (2) no role='alert' or aria-live — SR users get no announcement of the error state. (3) hardcoded colors #050505/#e0e0e0/#1a1a1a/#333 ignore theme. (4) 'Clear Data & Reload' wipes all localStorage (license, tour state, language, theme, IGDB creds) in a single click — no confirmation pattern despite katadelete having one.
findings.fix: Add role='alert' aria-live='assertive' to container. Use CSS vars for colors. i18n the strings with try/catch fallback (i18n itself might be what crashed). Two-click confirm for Clear Data.

findings.title: HoldButton has triple announcement channels
findings.affected_users: Screen reader users removing games from kata
findings.description: During remove-hold: (a) aria-label changes between static and confirm, (b) sr-only role='status' aria-live='assertive' for confirming state, (c) sr-only role='status' aria-live='assertive' for isRunning state. NVDA can announce 3 times on one state transition. Note: this component shows mature NVDA awareness at line 104-117 (detects NVDA browse-mode short-press and switches to two-step confirm) — the live-region redundancy is the only smell.
findings.fix: Consolidate to one aria-live='polite' region keyed to state ternary. Keep aria-label static.

findings.title: Tauri minWidth 1024 sidesteps 320px reflow but not 200% zoom
findings.affected_users: Users zooming to 200% (effective viewport 512px)
findings.description: minWidth 1024 means the window never renders below 1024 CSS px, bypassing the 320px reflow test. But 200% browser zoom halves effective CSS viewport to 512px. Reflow contract still matters at zoom levels.
findings.fix: Option A: Verify layout at 200% zoom, fix breakpoints. Option B: Document in AccessibilityPage 'Known Limitations' that Maida targets screens ≥ 1024px and zoom ≤ 150%. Both are legally defensible for desktop apps.

positives: prefers-reduced-motion supported across 7 CSS files
prefers-reduced-transparency supported (rare; even W3C WAI reference sites don't always have this)
:focus-visible used 24 times across 6 files (consistent focus discipline)
F1 chosen over H for help to avoid NVDA/JAWS heading-navigation collision (explicit code comment shows awareness)
<html lang> set before React hydration with locale-aware resolution
ErrorBoundary wraps app at root
Loading state uses role=status aria-live=polite
Two-step confirmation on destructive delete (Kata delete ×→?→delete)
Focus restoration after TracePanel / Legal pages close (requestAnimationFrame pattern)
aria-describedby linking inputs to help text (IGDB client id)
All form inputs have htmlFor labels
role=switch on telemetry toggle (modern pattern)
AccessibilityPage exists with structured sections (commitment, standards, features, limitations, planned, contact)
Gamepad support as motor-accessibility alternative (real win for users who can't do precise keyboard shortcuts)
Skip/Prev/Next button order in tour is stable
Onboarding places focus on h1 first so SR announces title before button
Kata select button aria-label includes game count (localized)
Haptic feedback (vibration) during long-press for cross-sensory confirmation
Space block is intentional (not negligent) even though misguided — code comments show the team thought about it
Tauri minWidth likely set — need to verify in tauri.conf.json

jurisdictions.name: US ADA Title III
jurisdictions.detail: 1 Level A (Space key block), 1 Level A dialog-no-trap. No overlay widgets. No CAPTCHAs. Accessibility statement exists. 8,600+ web lawsuits per year but desktop apps less targeted than commercial websites.

jurisdictions.name: EU EAA (European Accessibility Act)
jurisdictions.detail: Effective June 28, 2025. Applies to commercial products. Maida is currently free/MIT — check if released commercially later. B2C software products covered.

jurisdictions.name: Japan JIS X 8341-3
jurisdictions.law: Japanese Industrial Standard for web accessibility
jurisdictions.detail: JIS X 8341-3:2016 aligns with WCAG 2.0. Voluntary compliance. Maida's JP locale support (i18n jp) is a plus.

jurisdictions.name: Taiwan accessibility
jurisdictions.detail: Voluntary for private apps. Public-sector only.

remediation.title: Document Space block behavior in AccessibilityPage (keep code as-is)
remediation.note: Originally p0; reclassified after confirming intentional NVDA browse-mode defense

remediation.title: Fix role=alert spam in RinView + KamaeView
Add Tab focus trap to GuidedTour dialog
Make spotlight hint reachable by SR
Kata delete confirm feedback via aria-live
Convert px font vars to rem
Guard console.log in useGameInput with DEV check
Extend formatHotkeys regex for en/ja quotes

testing_recommendations: Tier 2: Start vite dev server, inject axe-core via Playwright, verify no additional violations beyond findings above

Tier 3: Manual NVDA walkthrough of full v0.2.0 flow: Onboarding -> Rin (game pick) -> Try/Not Now -> Anchor (long press Enter) -> Face switch to Kamae -> Kata create/rename/delete -> Settings (F10) -> Tour replay -> back to Rin
Tier 3: Manual keyboard-only walkthrough verifying Tab order, focus-visible indicators, Escape behavior
Tier 3: Gamepad-only walkthrough on Xbox controller verifying all functionality reachable without keyboard
Unit tests: Add jest-axe to existing Vitest setup, run on each component render snapshot
E2E: Playwright tests with keyboard-only interaction, assert focus state after each action
Before v0.3.0 release: re-run this audit, target overall_score >= 80
Before v1.0.0: Third-party audit (WebAIM, Deque) — commercial license justifies the cost
